expectations.text: States that Premium Bond prizes are tax-free
expectations.evidence: Line 20 explicitly states 'Premium Bond prizes | £500 | **No — completely tax-free**' and line 24 states 'Premium Bond prizes are entirely tax-free by law'
Line 22 states 'Total taxable savings interest: £1,600' and lines 65-66 show the correct sum in the summary table

expectations.text: Applies the PSA correctly (£1,000 for basic rate taxpayer at £48,000)
expectations.evidence: Lines 42-47 correctly identify employment income of £48,000, determine basic rate taxpayer status, and apply PSA of £1,000

expectations.text: Calculates tax due on £600 excess interest above PSA
expectations.evidence: Line 71 correctly calculates 'Tax due on savings interest: £600 x 20% = £120'

expectations.text: Explains that banks pay interest gross since 2016 (no automatic tax deduction)
expectations.evidence: Lines 32-34 state 'Since April 2016, banks and building societies no longer deduct tax at source. All interest is now paid gross'

expectations.text: Explains how HMRC collects the tax (tax code adjustment or Self Assessment)
expectations.evidence: Lines 34 and 81-82 explain both tax code adjustment ('adjusting your PAYE tax code') and Self Assessment as collection mechanisms
